This is my story

A girl from Delhi who bet on herself.

I was born and raised in Delhi, India — a city that gave me grit, speed, and an unshakeable belief that hard work pays off. Growing up, I was drawn to things that demanded both discipline and heart: semi-classical music on stage, volleyball, and eventually the National Cadet Corps.

The NCC made me. Rising to Sergeant — second in command of 51 cadets — I learnt that real leadership is mostly quiet service. Then came KPMG, two years of professional excellence, and a growing sense that I was ready for more.

So I packed everything and flew to Dublin, Ireland to study MSc Management Consultancy at UCD Smurfit. This is the story of that decision — and everything that followed.

Read the full journey →
The Ireland Chapter

How I came
to Dublin.

"Leaving India wasn't just a flight. It was a decision to bet on myself — to trade comfort for growth and everything I knew for the chance to become someone I hadn't met yet."

I chose Ireland because UCD Smurfit offered exactly the intellectual rigour I was looking for, in a country that felt connected to the world without being overwhelming.

What surprised me most was finding community in unexpected places — a part-time job at Woww Food, a small Indian café in Dublin, kept me anchored while my head was full of strategy frameworks and consulting models.

And sitting in a room at Crowe Ireland as part of a real consulting engagement — barely months into my programme — showed me exactly why I came.

01
Why Ireland?

UCD Smurfit — world-class rigour in management consultancy, in a country small enough to feel human but connected enough to matter globally.

02
What I Didn't Expect

Working at Woww Food café while studying. Serving chai on rainy mornings kept me grounded while my head filled with strategy and diagnostic frameworks.

03
What Dublin Taught Me

Being the only Indian woman in many rooms is not a disadvantage. It is a perspective nobody else has. Starting over is not failure — sometimes it is the most strategic move.

The goodbye at Delhi airport — the hardest and most important moment
First days at UCD — the leap made real
Holi in Dublin — finding home away from home
Academic & Professional Projects

Work that
proves it.

Diagnostic Techniques · UCD Smurfit · 2026
Crowe Ireland — Consulting Diagnostic Project

As part of the MSc in Management Consultancy, our team was engaged by Crowe Ireland — a leading Audit, Tax and Advisory firm — to evaluate and improve their internal project management environment.

Our work included a current state assessment of Crowe's SharePoint-based document management system, national and international benchmarking of professional services firms, a gap analysis, and a final implementation roadmap presented to Crowe's Operational Management Team.

The project demanded curiosity, a grounded approach, and the ability to translate research into actionable, client-ready recommendations — exactly the kind of work I came to Smurfit to do.

Stakeholder Engagement Benchmarking Gap Analysis SharePoint Implementation Roadmap Client Deliverable
Competitive Strategy · UCD Smurfit · 2026
Movado Group — "Revamp & Refocus" Strategy

Developed a full strategic analysis and repositioning recommendation for Movado Group — a Swiss luxury watchmaker squeezed between smartwatch disruption and high-end competitors.

Our team proposed a "Revamp & Refocus" strategy: repositioning Movado, Ebel and Concord as distinct high-value brands through brand identity clarity, portfolio rationalisation (from 380 to 20 products), and digital transformation. Goal: achieve 60% in-house brand sales by 2030.

Analysis included PESTEL, Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, Value Chain and a full opportunity analysis — delivered as a professional strategy poster and report.

PESTEL Analysis Porter's Five Forces SWOT Brand Strategy Digital Transformation Luxury Market
Beyond the Audit Work

Inclusion, Diversity
& Equity at KPMG

While delivering 100% on-time across every audit engagement, I made time for something I genuinely cared about: co-leading the Inclusion, Diversity & Equity (IDE) Committee at KPMG Global Services.

This wasn't an add-on to my role. I planned, organised and MC'd firm-wide events — bringing together hundreds of colleagues across different teams, seniority levels and backgrounds. I designed formats that felt human, not corporate. Events that people actually wanted to be at.

The work sharpened skills that no audit module teaches: event project management, stakeholder communication, public facilitation, and how to hold a room when the topic matters.

"The best way to understand inclusion is to practise it — not study it. Standing at the front of a room, making everyone feel seen, taught me more about leadership than any handbook."
The Timeline

The road that
got me here.

2019 – 2022
Sergeant — National Cadet Corps
NCC Group Chandigarh · India · Military Youth Leadership
  • Rose to Sergeant — second in command of a 51-cadet unit
  • Led training sessions, residential camps and discipline drills
  • Awarded Best Cadet & received Appreciation Certificate at NCC Group Chandigarh
What I took from this
"Real leadership is mostly quiet service. The NCC built the version of me who walks into hard situations without flinching."
Receiving my Appreciation Certificate · NCC Group Chandigarh
2019 – 2022
BSc Biotechnology
Delhi University · India
  • 3rd place, International Biotechnology Conference — scientific writing
  • Built a research-first, evidence-driven mindset that travels everywhere with me
What I took from this
"Science taught me to question assumptions before accepting any answer. That habit made me a better analyst and a better consultant."
March 2023 – August 2025
Audit Analyst — KPMG Global Services
Delhi, India · Financial Services & Assurance
  • Joined as the youngest on the team — proved my place fast
  • 100% on-time delivery across every engagement for two years
  • Reduced issue-resolution turnaround by 20% through process improvements
  • Cut onboarding ramp-up time by 30% through better training design
  • Co-led the Inclusion, Diversity & Equity (IDE) Committee — planned, hosted and MC'd firm-wide events
  • Designed onboarding programmes and delivered training sessions for new joiners
  • Awarded 1st Place, Content Quality Review, DGNCC Digital Forum
What I took from this
"KPMG taught me that professional excellence is less about being the smartest and more about being the most reliable. And it taught me I wanted something more — that's what got me on the plane."
2025 – Present
MSc Management Consultancy
UCD Michael Smurfit Graduate Business School · Dublin, Ireland
  • Studying Competitive Strategy, Digital Transformation, Change Management & Organisational Behaviour
  • Engaged on a live consulting project with Crowe Ireland — Diagnostic Techniques module
  • Delivered Movado Group competitive strategy analysis — PESTEL, Porter's Five Forces, SWOT
  • Applying PM frameworks — WBS, Gantt, RACI, Risk Analysis — to real projects
  • Working part-time at Woww Food, a local Indian café in Dublin
What I'm learning right now
"The Masters is teaching me to think at a different altitude — to see systems, not just tasks. Working at the café keeps me honest. Both things are true at once."
What I Bring

Core
capabilities.

🎯
Structured Problem Solving

Root cause first, fix second — always. I diagnose before I prescribe, whether it's an audit gap or a consulting brief.

🤝
Stakeholder Management

Cadets to corporate clients — years of earning trust at every level and translating what leadership wants into what teams can execute.

📊
Data & Financial Analysis

IFRS, GAAP, financial control environments. Rigorous, hypothesis-driven analysis that leads to decisions, not just observations.

🚀
Execution Under Pressure

100% on-time at KPMG. Built by the NCC before audit seasons ever tested it. Calm is a skill I've practised for years.

🌍
Cross-Cultural Intelligence

From Delhi to Dublin — I adapt quickly and bring perspectives to rooms that most people in those rooms simply don't have.

📣
Facilitation & L&D

Designed onboarding, delivered training, MC'd IDE events for hundreds. I know how to make a room feel alive and learning stick.

Beyond the Desk

The life that
built me.

01
🏐
Volleyball Competitor

School tournaments across India. The sport taught me how to read a team in real time, when to lead and when to set someone else up for the winning point. Those instincts come into every group project and every client room.

02
🎵
Semi-Classical Singer

Performed in school competitions — technical precision and emotional presence simultaneously. It changed how I think about communication: the words matter, but delivery is everything.

03
🎖️
NCC Sergeant

Second in command of 51 cadets. Best Cadet award. The NCC built the version of me who walks into hard situations without flinching and stays calm when everything else is loud.

04
🍛
Woww Food — Dublin

Working part-time at a small Indian café while studying for my MSc. It keeps me grounded — and reminds me that the best consultants never lose touch with how real operations actually run.
